basic_info.name: Seminyak Beach / 水明漾海灘 / Pantai Seminyak (includes Double Six)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Canggu ~15 min, Uluwatu ~40 min by scooter

surf_conditions.wave_type: Beach break, sand-bottom; multiple shifting left and right peaks. Double Six is a notable high-tide longboard-friendly section
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate; small days suit beginners with supervision
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-6 ft typical; up to 8 ft on solid SW swells with hollow sections
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season (April/May-October) for offshore winds and groundswells
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide; low tide produces heavy close-outs
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore from E/NE/N (near-offshore to cross-offshore due to curved coastline); SE trade winds are favorable; early morning is best
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to S groundswells (primary); also works on W swell
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 40-80 m on good sandbar days; Double Six offers longer 60-100 m rides on high tide for longboards
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: medium; can be fast and hollow on bigger SW swells
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate; punchier than Kuta/Legian with steeper drops and close-out potential on bigger days; sand bottom keeps consequences low
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Multiple shifting peaks along the stretch; Double Six is the most consistent and is suited to longboarders on high tide; outside peaks offer steeper drops
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 3-4 (less crowded than Kuta but busy at Double Six and around upscale beach clubs)

practical_info.access: Walk-on beach access via Jalan Double Six, Jalan Camplung Tanduk, and side streets through beach clubs (Ku De Ta, Potato Head, La Plancha)
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Walk-out from sand through whitewater; duck-dive inside waves; on bigger days use rip channels between sandbars
practical_info.entry_fee: Free beach access; scooter parking IDR 5,000-10,000, car parking IDR 10,000-20,000 (premium near beach clubs)
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 AM) for offshore winds and cleanest conditions; sunset session highly popular for atmosphere and lighting
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Before 8 AM to avoid surf-school crowds and onshore wind; sunset for ambience but more crowded
practical_info.nearby_warung: Numerous warungs along Jalan Camplung Tanduk; upscale beach clubs (Ku De Ta, Potato Head, La Plancha, Mrs Sippy) and cafés directly on the beach
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Legian / Double Six (5 min south), Petitenget / Batu Belig (5 min north), Berawa / Canggu (15 min north)
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: [uncertain] Functions as base for west-coast day-trips; not a typical multi-island combo base

safety_culture.hazards: Strong rip currents (especially on bigger swells), close-out sections on low tide, occasional jet-ski traffic and banana boats, swimmers and sunbathers in the lineup zone
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Occasional jellyfish (wet season), rare blue-bottles, no reef or urchins; treat stings with vinegar and warm water
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong rips form between sandbars; identifiable as darker calmer channels of seaward-flowing water. Seminyak rips can be powerful on bigger swells - swim parallel to shore to escape
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Standard right-of-way rules; respect Double Six longboarders on high tide; no drop-ins; share peaks; smile and say hi
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Respect beach-club zones; do not paddle directly in front of swimmer/sunbather zones; tip parking guards; pick up trash
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: [uncertain] No iconic individual locals specifically tied to Seminyak; many Bali pros surf the area
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Petitenget directly adjacent to north end of Seminyak Beach - respect ceremonial offerings (canang sari) on the sand; Nyepi (March) closes the beach; melasti processions pass through
safety_culture.medical_nearby: BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua/Kuta, Siloam Hospitals Denpasar, Kasih Ibu Hospital Kuta, Unicare Clinic Seminyak
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Balawista lifeguards on duty with rescue boards; jet-ski may be available in some sections
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Balawista Bali Lifeguard: +62 361 755660; Ambulance: 118; Police: 110; BIMC Kuta: +62 361 761263

services.nearest_surf_school: Odyssey Surf School Seminyak, Rip Curl School of Surf, Bali Green Surf School, Bali Diamond Surf - all within walking distance
services.board_rental_availability: Widely available beachfront and from surf shops on Jalan Camplung Tanduk; IDR 100,000-200,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes, freelance land-based photographers along the beach (especially at Double Six)
services.photographer_recommendation: [uncertain] Various freelance beach photographers; typical rate IDR 300,000-600,000 per session/package
services.accommodation_options: The Legian Bali, W Bali - Seminyak, The Oberoi Beach Resort, Anantara Seminyak Resort, plus many mid-range hotels and villas; surf-focused stays like Mick's Place are nearby
services.drone_allowed: Generally yes for recreational use; avoid flying over beach clubs and crowds; commercial use requires permit
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Seminyak (~5 min), Tropical Nomad (Canggu ~15 min), Dojo Bali (Canggu); Outpost is the main coworking option in Seminyak

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 450,000-750,000 (USD 28-48 / TWD 900-1,550) per 2-hour group lesson (premium positioning vs Kuta)
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 100,000-200,000 (USD 6-13) per day for soft-tops/shortboards; longboards IDR 150,000-250,000
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: [uncertain] Usually included with lesson; standalone rental ~IDR 50,000-75,000/day
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: [uncertain] Small ding repair IDR 100,000-400,000; multiple ding-repair workshops in Seminyak/Kerobokan
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: [uncertain] Limited dedicated surf camps directly in Seminyak; nearby camps from USD 800-1,500 for 7-day packages
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: [uncertain] Typical: accommodation, breakfast, daily lessons, board use, airport transfer, video analysis, yoga sessions
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: [uncertain] Peak season (Jul-Aug, Dec-Jan) ~20-30% higher; Seminyak has higher peak-season premium than Kuta due to upscale positioning

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Moderate to poor in wet season (Nov-Mar) due to runoff and plastic pollution; acceptable in dry season; slightly better than Kuta due to less dense urban runoff but still affected by Bali-wide rainy-season debris
sustainability.eco_certification: [uncertain] No spot-level certification; some beach clubs hold sustainability programs
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: [uncertain] Not standard at the spot level

events_community.annual_competition_window: [uncertain] Hosts occasional longboard contests and beach festivals (Double Six area); fewer pro-level events than Bukit reefs
events_community.community_events: Sunset gatherings at La Plancha and Ku De Ta; Single Fin Sundays (Uluwatu) draws regional surfers; informal Double Six longboard meet-ups
